Major sources of ALP in plasma are liver and bone. ALP is also elevated in pregnancy. GI conditions, gynaecological malignancies, infarct of solid organs and severe infection. ALP heat stability index and other liver enzymes such as GGT can help determine the source of elevated ALP.

Anti-skin autoantibodies have 2 patterns, ICS and DEJ. The intercellular substance (ICS) pattern is found in pemphigus vulgaris and the titre is useful for monitoring the disease. The dermo-epidermal junction (DEJ) pattern is found in bullous pemphigoid but is not useful for monitorin.

Useful in the differential diagnosis of autoimmune hepatitis (lupoid hepatitis) from extrahepatic biliary obstruction, drug induced liver disease, viral hepatitis and other conditions involving the liver.

For differential diagnosis of autoimmune hepatitis (lupoid hepatitis) from extrahepatic biliary obstruction, drug induced liver disease, viral hepatitis and other conditions involving the liver. Persistent high titre (1:80 - 1:320) is highly suggestive of autoimmune hepatitis (types 1, 3 & 4).
